why.heading: The difference you'll actually feel
why.subheading: Not just another agency promise — tangible commitments built on five years of real project experience.
why.i1Desc: Every project is documented, tested, and structured so any developer can take it over. No proprietary lock-in, no spaghetti.
why.i2Desc: You work directly with me — no account managers, no layers. You always know exactly where your project stands.
why.i3Desc: I set deadlines I can actually keep. If anything changes, you hear from me first — not the day before launch.
why.i4Desc: I stay involved after delivery. Monitoring, fixes, and iterations are part of every engagement — not paid extras.

tech.heading: Tools I ship with
tech.subheading: A modern, battle-tested stack chosen for performance, scalability, and long-term maintainability.

faq.heading: Common questions
faq.subheading: Everything you need to know before we start working together.
faq.q1: What is your typical project timeline?
faq.a1: It depends on scope. A landing page takes 2–3 weeks; a full web application typically 6–12 weeks. I give you a detailed, itemised estimate after our discovery call — no guesswork.
faq.q2: Do you work with startups or established companies?
faq.a2: Both. I've built MVPs for early-stage founders and shipped production features for companies with millions of users. The process and communication style adapt to your stage.
faq.q3: What does the maintenance package include?
faq.a3: Monthly dependency updates, priority bug fixes, uptime & error monitoring, security patches, and a dedicated support channel — all for a fixed monthly fee agreed upfront.
faq.q4: How do you handle revisions and feedback?
faq.a4: Every project has structured revision rounds built into the timeline. Feedback is collected asynchronously and addressed in focused sessions — not scattered, endless back-and-forth.
faq.a5: Both are available. Fixed price suits well-defined projects. Hourly or retainer billing works better for ongoing work, evolving scopes, or long-term partnerships.
faq.q6: Can you work with an existing codebase or design?
faq.a6: Yes. I start with a code or design audit to understand what's already there, then integrate cleanly. I work comfortably in any modern JavaScript or TypeScript stack.
faq.q7: What happens after the project launches?
faq.a7: Every project includes a 30-day post-launch support window at no extra cost. After that, you can move to a maintenance retainer or reach out on a per-request basis.
faq.a8: Send a short message describing your project. We book a free 30-minute discovery call, I prepare a detailed proposal, and we kick off from there — usually within a week.

cta.heading: Ready to get started?
cta.subheading: Book a free 30-minute discovery call. No commitment, no pressure — just clarity on what your project needs.
